info.description:
Welcome to the Gr4vy API reference documentation.
Our API is still very much a work in product and subject to change.
info.title: Gr4vy API (Beta)

400.description: Returns an error if the request was badly formatted or missing required fields.

401.description: Returns an error if no valid authentication was provided.

post.description: Stores and tokenizes a new payment method

400.description: Returns an error if the request was badly formatted.

401.description: Returns an error if no valid authentication was provided.

post.description:
Attempts to create an authorization for a payment method.

This endpoint accepts a few additional fields that allow for simultaneous
capturing and storage of the payment method.

* `payment_method.store` - Use this field to store the payment method for
future use. Not all payment methods support this feature.
* `capture` - Use this method to also perform a capture of the transaction
after it has been authorized.

400.description: Returns an error if the request was badly formatted.

401.description: Returns an error if no valid authentication was provided.

post.description:
The body of this API takes a list of items as a JSON object.

This API returns asynchronously, returning a list of status objects with the
IDs of the pending `Transaction` resources.

post.description:

This API returns asynchronously, returning status object with the ID of the
pending `Transaction` resource.

parameters.description: The ID of the payment method
parameters.name: payment_method_id

302.description:
Redirects the browser back to the `redirect_url` specified
when the payment method was first created. It appends the payment method's
ID and status.

location.description:
The URL to redirect the browser to. This is the `redirect_url`
specified when the payment method was first created with some
additional query parameters appended.

* `payment_method_id` - The ID of the payment method
* `payment_method_status` - The current value of the
  `payment_method_status`  field of the payment method

404.description: Returns an error if the resource can not be found.

get.description:
Internal API used as a redirect endpoint for payment methods that require
buyer authorization.

For example, when a buyer tries to add a PayPal payment method, the buyer
needs to be sent to PayPal, after which they are sent back to this endpoint
upon completion.

This API applies any required updates for the payment method based on its
query parameters and then redirects the browser back to the `redirect_url`
specified when the payment method was first created.

post.description:
Activates a ruleset for card transactions, making it the one
ruleset used by all card transactions.

Only one ruleset can be active at any time. By activating a ruleset the
existing active ruleset will be deactivated.

Only valid rulesets can be activated.

parameters.description:
The marker that determines the offset for the page of results to be
returned.

For the first page of results, omit this parameter. For next pages,
use the value returned by the API in the `next_page` field.

A marker is an opaque string that is only meaningful to our API. No
additional value can be ascribed to this value.

post.description:
Attempts to create an authorization for a payment method. In some cases it is
not possible to create the authorization without redirecting the user for
their authorization. In these cases the status is set to
`buyer_authorization_required` and an `authorization_url` is returned.

Additionally, this endpoint accepts a few additional fields that allow for
simultaneous capturing and storage of the payment method.

* `store` - Use this field to store the payment method for
future use. Not all payment methods support this feature.
* `capture` - Use this method to also perform a capture of the transaction
after it has been authorized.

parameters.description: The ID for the transaction to get the information for.
parameters.name: transaction_id

404.description: Returns an error if the resource can not be found or has not yet been created.

post.description:
Refunds or voids transaction. If this transaction was already captured, it
will issue a refund. If the transaction was not yet captured the authorization
will instead be voided.

created_at.description:
The date and time
when this buyer was created in our system.

external_identifier.description: An external identifier that can be used to match the buyer against your own records.

id.description: The unique Gr4vy ID for this buyer

type.description: The type of this resource. Is always `buyer`

updated_at.description:
The date and time
when this buyer was last updated in our system.

display_name.description: A unique name for this buyer which is used in the Gr4vy admin panel to give a buyer a human readable name.

external_identifier.description: An external identifier that can be used to match the buyer against your own records. This value needs to be unique for all buyers.

external_identifier.description: An external identifier that can be used to match the buyer against your own records.

created_at.description:
The date and time when this payment method was first created in our
system.

external_identifier.description:
An external identifier that can be used to match the payment method
against your own records.

id.description:
The optional ID of the payment method details. This will be `null` if the
payment method was not tokenized on only used in a one-off transaction.

payment_method_status.description: The state of the card tokenization.

status.description: The state of the payment method in Gr4vy.

updated_at.description: The date and time when this payment method was last updated in our system.

CardDetails.description: Additional details about a stored card

expiration_date.description: The expiration date for a card.

number.description:
Partial number details for a stored card, hiding all but the last few
numbers.

CardDetails.title: Card details

CardRuleConditional.description:
A conditional rule in a ruleset. It defines an if/else condition and a rule to
move on to next for each of these values.

condition.description: The comparison to make to `value` property.

false_rule_id.description: the ID of the rule to use if the comparison equals false.

id.description: A unique ID for this rule in the ruleset

key.description: The transaction field to filter by

true_rule_id.description: the ID of the rule to use if the comparison equals true.

value.description:
The value to compare the `key` to. This value will be converted to
a number for the currency amount.

CardRuleConditional.title: Conditional Card Rule

CardRuleTerminal.description:
A terminal rule in a ruleset. It the payment services to use in case the
ruleset reaches this node.

id.description: A unique ID for this rule in the ruleset

payment_service_ids.description:
A list of IDs for the payment services to use, in order of priority.
The payment services all need to process cards.

CardRuleTerminal.title: Terminal Card Rule

CardRuleset.description:
A card ruleset which holds the rules that are parsed for a card transaction.

Multiple rulesets can exists at any time, but only one can be deployed.

active.description:
Whether this ruleset is currently in use. Only one ruleset can
be active at any time.

created_at.description: The date and time when this ruleset was created

display_name.description: The display name of the ruleset

root_rule_id.description: The ID of the rule that is the starting point of the ruleset.

rules.description: A list of rules in this ruleset

updated_at.description: The date and time when this ruleset was last updated

valid.description:
Whether this ruleset is valid and complete. Only valid rulesets can be
activated.

CardRuleset.title: Card Ruleset

CardRulesetCreateRequest.description:
Creates a new card ruleset which holds the rules that are parsed for a card
transaction.

display_name.description: The display name of the ruleset

root_rule_id.description: The ID of the rule that is the starting point of the ruleset.

rules.description: A list of rules in this ruleset

CardRulesetCreateRequest.title: Card Ruleset (Create)

CardRulesetUpdateRequest.description:
Updates a card ruleset which holds the rules that are parsed for a card
transaction.

display_name.description: The display name of the ruleset

root_rule_id.description: The ID of the rule that is the starting point of the ruleset.

rules.description: A list of rules in this ruleset

CardRulesetUpdateRequest.title: Card Ruleset (Update)

limit.description: The limit set to the number of items returned.

next_page.description: The marker that represents the next page of results.

previous_page.description: The marker that represents the previous page of results.

CardStoreRequest.description: A request to store a card.

buyer_external_identifier.description:
The `external_identifier` of the buyer to associate this payment method
to. This field is ignored if the `buyer_id` is provided.

buyer_id.description:
The ID of the buyer to associate this payment method to. If provided, the
`buyer_external_identifier` is ignored.

expiration_date.description:
The expiration date of the card, formatted `MM/YY`. If a card has been
previously stored with us this value is optional.

If the `number` of this card represents a tokenized card, then this value
is ignored.

external_identifier.description: An external identifier that can be used to match the card against your own records.

number.description:
The 15-16 digit number for this credit card as it can be found on the
front of the card.

If a card has been stored with us previously, this number will represent
the unique tokenized card ID provided via our API.

security_code.description:
The 3 or 4 digit security code often found on the card. This often
referred to as the CVV or CVD.

If the `number` of this card represents a tokenized card, then this value
is ignored.

CardStoreRequest.title: Card (Store)

code.description: A custom code to further describe the type of error being returned. This code provides further specification within the HTTP `status` code and can be used by a program to define logic based on the error.

message.description: A human readable message that describes the error. The content of this field should not be used to determine any business logic.
message.example: The request body was not valid JSON.

status.description: The HTTP status code of this error

type.description: The type of this object. This is always `error`.

message.description:
Describes the fields that are missing or incorrectly formatted in the API
request.
Incorrect JSON. The request body could not be parsed as valid JSON.
message.example: Incorrect JSON. The request body could not be parsed as valid JSON.

message.description: The resource could not be found
enum: The resource could not be found
message.example: The resource could not be found

message.description: Further details on the field that triggered the error.
message.example: A duplicate ecord exists with this external_identifier value

display_name.description: The name of this merchant as it will be displayed in the admin UI.

allOf.description: The optional buyer for which this payment method has been stored.

created_at.description:
The date and time when this payment method was first created in our
system.

external_identifier.description:
An external identifier that can be used to match the payment method
against your own records.

id.description:
The optional ID of the payment method details. This will be `null` if the
payment method was not tokenized on only used in a one-off transaction.

payment_method_status.description:
The state of the account tokenization. After the first call this will
be set to `buyer_authorization_required` and the response will include
an `authorization_url`. The buyer needs to be redirected to this URL
to authorize the future payments. The token is subsequently posted to
the [authorize](/#operation/authorize-payment-method) endpoint
after which the `status` is updated to `stored`.

status.description: The state of the payment method in Gr4vy.

updated_at.description: The date and time when this payment method was last updated in our system.

PayPalDetails.description: Additional details about a stored PayPal account

authorization_url.description: The optional URL that the buyer needs to be redirected to to further authorize their PayPal payments.

email_address.description: The email address associated to the PayPal account.

PayPalDetails.title: PayPal account details

PayPalStoreRequest.description: A request to tokenize a PayPal account.

buyer_external_identifier.description:
The `external_identifier` of the buyer to associate this payment method
to. This field is ignored if the `buyer_id` is provided.

buyer_id.description:
The ID of the buyer to associate this payment method to. If provided, the
`buyer_external_identifier` is ignored.

external_identifier.description: An external identifier that can be used to match the account against your own records.

redirect_url.description:
The redirect URL to redirect a buyer to after they have authorized their
PayPal transaction.

PayPalStoreRequest.title: PayPal Account (Store)

status.description:

to created, and might eventually move to an error status if and when the

use_test_environment.description: Defines if this payment service uses the service's test or sandbox environment. Defaults to false.

previous_page.description: The marker that represents the previous page of results.

PaymentServiceDefinitions.title: Payment service definitions

PaymentServiceUpdateRequest.description: Request body for updating an active payment service

accepted_countries.description: A list of countries that this payment service needs to support in ISO two-letter code format.

accepted_currencies.description: A list of currencies that this payment service needs to support in ISO 4217 three-letter code format.

display_name.description: A custom name for the payment service. This will be shown in the Admin UI.

fields.description: A list of fields, each containing a key-value pair for each field defined by the definition for this payment service.

items.description: A field containing a key-value pair for a required field defined by the service for this payment service.

key.description: The key of the field to set a value for.

value.description: The value of a field to set.

use_test_environment.description: Defines if this payment service uses the service's test/sandbox environment.

PaymentServiceUpdateRequest.title: Payment Service (Update)

PaymentServices.description: A list of active, configured payment services

limit.description: The limit set to the number of items returned.

next_page.description: The marker that represents the next page of results.

previous_page.description: The marker that represents the previous page of results.

PaymentServices.title: A list of active payment services

Status.description: In many cases the Gr4vy API returns asynchronously, kicking off a job to create an authorization or a transaction, and returning a `Status` object with the ID of the pending object. The ID of this resource can be used to query an objects status, or additionally the client can use Pub/Sub to subscribe to the creation of the pending object.

external_identifier.description: An external identifier that can be used to match the record against your own records.

resource_id.description: The ID of the object for which this status has been created.

resource_type.description: The type of the object that is pending.

status.description:
The status of this resource being created. This can be one of:

* `pending` - The creation has not yet completed
* `created` - The resource has been created and can now be queried.

type.description: The type of this object. This is always `status`.

Status.title: Status

amount.description: The currency amount captured by this transaction.

created_at.description: The date and time when this transaction was created in our system.

currency.description: The currency code for this transaction.

external_identifier.description: An external identifier that can be used to match the transaction against your own records.

id.description: The unique identifier for this transaction

payment_service_id.description: The ID of the payment service that was used for this transaction.

status.description: Whether this transaction was successfully created or not.

transaction_status.description: The status of the transaction being processed. This is different from the `status` field in that it represents the status of the transaction at the payment processor, not the status of the transaction created in Gr4vy.

type.description: The type of this resource. Is always `transaction`

updated_at.description: Defines when the transaction was last updated

TransactionCaptureRequest.description: A request to capture a transaction

amount.description:
The (partial) amount to capture.

When left blank, this will capture the entire amount.

external_identifier.description: An external identifier that can be used to match the transaction against your own records.

TransactionCaptureRequest.title: Capture a transaction

TransactionCreateRequest.description: A request to create a transaction

amount.description:
The monetary amount to create an authorization for, in the smallest
currency unit for the given currency, for example `1299` cents to create
an authorization for `$12.99`.

For BitCoin, this value will be a decimal point number, for example
`0.0039`.

capture.description: Whether a successful authorization should immediately be captured as well, effectively processing the payment directly.

external_identifier.description: An external identifier that can be used to match the transaction against your own records.

payment_method.description: The optional payment method details to create an authorization for. This field is required for processing a card.

store.description:
Whether or not to also try and store the payment method with us so that
it can be used again for future use.

use_test_environment.description: Defines if this transaction should run in the payment provider's test environment. Defaults to false.

TransactionCreateRequest.title: Transaction (Create)

TransactionEvent.description: An event that happened for a transaction.

code.description: The type of event, in dot-format syntax.

id.description: The unique ID for this event.

resource.description: An optional related resource that triggered this event.

display_name.description: The user's name for the resource

TransactionEvent.title: Transaction Event

TransactionEvents.description: A list of events for a transaction

limit.description: The limit set to the number of items returned.

next_page.description: The marker that represents the next page of results.

previous_page.description: The marker that represents the previous page of results.

TransactionEvents.title: Transaction Events

limit.description: The limit set to the number of items returned.

next_page.description: The marker that represents the next page of results.

previous_page.description: The marker that represents the previous page of results.

TransactionsBatchCaptureRequest.description: A request to capture multiple previously authorized transactions.

transaction_id.description: The ID of the transaction to capture

TransactionsBatchCaptureRequest.title: Batch Capture (Create)

tags.description:
The asynchronous (async) APIs are convenience APIs that function as an
alternative way to create transactions and register payment methods
without having to wait for the API to complete. Each of thee APIs returns
directly without processing the request fully, allowing you to render a
new page for the buyer while the request is processed.
tags.name: Async APIs

tags.description:
In Gr4vy, a buyer represents your customer, the shopper who's performing
a checkout and making a purchase.

## Features

A buyer can be used by you to:

* Display a human readable name (`display_name`) for a buyer in the Gr4vy admin panel
* Associate multiple tokenized payment methods with a single user
* Initialize **Gr4vy Embed** with the buyer ID, automatically displaying the
  buyer's previously stored payment methods, allowing for faster checkout.
tags.name: Buyers

tags.description:
Callbacks are API endpoints used by Gr4vy for catching redirect callbacks
from payment providers.
tags.name: Callbacks

tags.description:
In Gr4vy, a card ruleset represents a set of rules that define how a card
transaction is processed. It determines which payment services are used,
and with what priority, based on the transaction amount, card type, and
many more attributes.

## Features

A card ruleset can be used to:

* Determine what payment service is used based on transaction and card
properties.
* Determine what fallback payment services to use if the transaction
did not succeed with the initial provider.
tags.name: Card Rulesets

tags.description:
In Gr4vy, a merchant represents the entity that owns the Gr4vy cluster.
Currently only one merchant is supported per cluster.
tags.name: Merchants

tags.description:
In Gr4vy, a payment method represents a way in which a payment can be processed,
for example a card payment, a PayPal payment, or a Bitcoin payment.

## Features

The payment method API can be used to:

* Determine the available payment methods in a cluster
* Determine the available payment method for a buyer in a specific currency and country
* Store (also known as tokenize) a payment method for a buyer
* Fetch all previously tokenized payment methods for a buyer
tags.name: Payment Methods

tags.description:
In Gr4vy, a payment option represents a list of methods (card, PayPal,
etc) that are available for a given locale.

## Features

The payment options API can be used to:

* Determine what types of payments can be processed in a specific locale
* Display a list options to a buyer to choose from
tags.name: Payment Options

tags.description:
Payment service definitions describe the fields required for a payment
service to be configured.
tags.name: Payment Service Definitions

tags.description:
In Gr4vy, a payment service represents a configured payment provider
(Stripe, PayPal, Adyen, etc) for a specific payment type (card, bitcoin,
etc)

## Features

The payment services API can be used to:

* Provide Gr4vy with the payment credentials for a payment provider
* Set a display name for a payment provider
* Prepare a payment provider for usage in a ruleset
tags.name: Payment Services

tags.description:
In Gr4vy, a transaction represents a payment in any state, either before it is
authorized, once it is captured, or after it has been refunded.

## Features

The transactions API can be used to:

- Authorize, capture, and store cards
- Authorize, capture, and store alternative payment methods like PayPal
- Refund, void, and otherwise cancel existing transactions
tags.name: Transactions
